content.problemStatement:
Your best friend has a very interesting necklace with nnn pearls. On each of the pearls of the necklace there is an integer. However, your friend wants to modify the necklace a bit and asks you for help. She wants to move the first pearl kkk spots to the left (and do so with all other pearls).

For example: if the necklace was originally 1,5,3,4,21, 5, 3, 4, 21,5,3,4,2 and k=2k = 2k=2, now it becomes 3,4,2,1,53, 4, 2, 1, 53,4,2,1,5.

Help your best friend determine how the necklace will look after the modification.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input, the first containing two integers n,kn, kn,k.
The second line of each test case contains nnn integers a1,a2,...,ana_1, a_2, ..., a_na1​,a2​,...,an​ representing the integers on the pearls starting from the first one.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line nnn integers representing the necklace after modification.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers n and k — the number of pearls and the number of positions to rotate left. The second line contains n integers a1 a2 … an, the values on the pearls in order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line with n integers — the necklace after rotating left by k positions.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: rotating left by 3 moves the first three elements to the end, giving 4 2 1 5 3. Second test case: rotating left by 5 (which is n‑1) moves the first five elements to the end, giving 2 10 1 2 9 8.
A single element array never changes regardless of k.
After rotating left by 2, the first two elements (10, -5) move to the end.

solution.approach: The required operation is a left rotation of the array by k positions. Because rotating by n yields the original array, we first replace k with k % n (if n > 0). The element that will appear at position i in the result is the original element at index (k + i) % n. We can either build a new array using this formula or directly output the elements in that order. This runs in O(n) time per test case and uses O(n) auxiliary space for the input array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing the input array (output can be streamed).

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        k = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        if n:
            k %= n
        rotated = arr[k:] + arr[:k]
        out.append(' '.join(map(str, rotated)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
